relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Tissue dissociation, organoids and iPDCs/Organoid dissociation.docx
full_text:
iPDC dissociation for flow cytometry using Dispase III

Collect the culture medium into a 1.5 ml Eppendorf (approx. 1200 ul from 4 wells), keep it aside, on ice. If there are different treatment conditions, collect them into separate tubes. Carefully aspirate the medium from the remaining wells using a suction pump.

Wash the wells 1x with cold sterile PBS.

Add 300uL/well DispaseII solution (Stock 50U, final concentration 1U/mL), prepared in tissue dissociation medium. 100ul of stock DISPASE in 4.9ml of tissue dissociation medium. Depending on the number of wells, calculate the volume of Dispase+medium. For example for 20 wells you need 6ml of Dispase+medium.

Incubate the plate in a cell culture incubator for 30 min.

During Dispase incubation time, spin the supernatant (collected at step 1.) at 1500 rpm for 10 min at +4C (Vähärautio centrifuge). Set the temperature of the cell culture centrifuge to +4C.

Collect the supernatant into a new clean Eppendorf, label the tube as Sample ID_iPDC_culture days(d6)_control/treated_date_initials. and store at -80C in a box named ONCOSYS-OVA cell culture supernatant -80C.

After the incubation time, bring the plate back into the laminar and break the ome-gel by pipetting up and down several times using a 1 mL pipet, transfer the content from the wells into 15 ml falcons: pool wells from the same condition. Centrifuge at 400g for 6 min at +4 (cell culture centrifuge).

Remove the supernatant from step 7, resuspend the pellet in 900uL of FACS staining buffer per tube. Centrifuge at 400g for 6 min at +4 (Vähärautio centrifuge). Proceed with staining protocol.
